title: approvals — validation rules
description: Six custom Laravel validation rules exported by ApprovalServiceProvider::boot(). Two are format-validators (valid_action_key, valid_expression, valid_selector_expression). Two are business-invariant guards (template_active_uniqueness, caller_is_approver). Two are constraint validators (min_reject_comment_length). Every rule is FormRequest-composable AND used internally by the observers as a defense-in-depth check.

rules.name: valid_action_key
rules.description: Validates a string as an approvable-action key: (a) matches `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*(\.[a-z][a-z0-9_]*)+$`, (b) exists in approvable_actions.action_key with is_active=true. Fails with `INVALID_ACTION_KEY_FORMAT` on regex mismatch OR `PLATFORM_ACTION_NOT_ACTIVE` on registry miss.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a valid, registered approvable action key.

rules.name: valid_expression
rules.description: Validates a string as a parseable ExpressionLanguage source. Runs via ExpressionLanguageAdapter::compile() with the built-in variable set + selector functions loaded. Fails with `INVALID_EXPRESSION` on parse error (returns the offending token position for template-authoring UI to highlight). Optional context_schema_id argument names an approvable-action's context_variables_schema — when present, the rule ADDITIONALLY validates that every variable referenced in the expression is declared in the schema (returns `UNKNOWN_EXPRESSION_VARIABLE`).

params.name: context_schema_id
params.description: Optional. When present, references an approvable_actions.context_variables_schema_json to validate referenced variable names.

rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a valid expression. Parse error: :error at position :position.

rules.name: valid_selector_expression
rules.description: Same as valid_expression BUT additionally validates the expression's return type is User[] OR User OR string (user_id). Rejects boolean-returning expressions (that's a when_expression, not a selector). Fails with `INVALID_SELECTOR_RETURN_TYPE`. Optional tenant_id arg is used at preflight — passes the caller's tenant so the parser resolves role() / user() references against the correct tenant scope.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a valid approver selector expression returning User[].

rules.name: template_active_uniqueness
rules.description: Guards the one-is-active invariant on ApprovalTemplate. When is_active=true is being submitted, verifies no other row exists with is_active=true AND matching (tenant_id, application_id, action_key, name). Optional except_id excludes the row being updated (self-check). Fails with `TEMPLATE_ACTIVE_UNIQUENESS_VIOLATION`. Defense-in-depth alongside ApprovalTemplateObserver which enforces the invariant at write-time.
rules.default_message: Another active template already exists for this tenant + application + action + name.

rules.name: caller_is_approver
rules.description: Security-critical. Validates that the current auth()->user()->id is in the approval_requirement.resolved_approvers array. Fails with `CALLER_NOT_APPROVER`. Runs BEFORE the ApprovalInstancePolicy check on approve/reject actions AND is enforced by the approvals.enforce_approver middleware AND is enforced by the ApprovalDecisionObserver. Three independent layers protect the same invariant — a policy misconfiguration can't open a forgery seam.
rules.default_message: You are not authorised to decide this approval requirement.

rules.name: min_reject_comment_length
rules.description: Enforces the minimum comment length on reject decisions (default 10 chars — from approvals.security.min_reject_reason_length). SOC 2 CC6.3 evidence requirement — every rejection must have a rationale attributable to a specific reviewer. Fails with `REJECT_COMMENT_TOO_SHORT`. Only applied when the parent form's `decision === 'reject'`.
rules.default_message: The rejection reason must be at least :min characters (SOC 2 evidence requirement).
